betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6BSCCO Superconducting Wire§f

§aBSCCO wire is the high-temperature superconductor for ring-accelerator-grade magnets.§f
The §bBSCCO Wire§f (`qmd:part:4`, ore dict `wireBSCCO`) is a high-temperature superconducting wire used to build §bBSCCO Accelerator Electromagnets§f, §bBSCCO RF Cavities§f, and §bVacuum Chamber Coils§f in QMD. BSCCO stands for §cBismuth Strontium Calcium Copper Oxide§f — a §bReBCO-family§f superconductor with critical temperatures above 100 K.

§6Recipe (graph: `hbm.assembly_machine` / `nuclearcraft_assembler`)§f:
Per `QMDRecipes.java:494-498` (assembler) and `HBM AssemblyMachine`:
- Input 1: §bBSCCO Dust§f (`qmd:chemical_dust:1`, 3 units)
- Input 2: §bSilver Ingot§f (`thermalfoundation:material:130` / `nuclearcraft:ingot:13`, 6 units)
- Input 3-4: empty
- Output: §bBSCCO Wire§f (`wireBSCCO`, 6 units)
- Process: §bHBM Assembly Machine§f §bor§f §bNC Assembler§f

§6BSCCO Dust Recipe (QMDRecipes.java:498)§f:
Per `NCRecipes.assembler.addRecipe(...)`:
- Input 1: §bBismuth Dust§f (`dustBismuth`, 2)
- Input 2: §bStrontium Dust§f (`dustStrontium`, 2)
- Input 3: §bCalcium Dust§f (`dustCalcium`, 2)
- Input 4: §bCopper Oxide Dust§f (`dustCopperOxide`, 3)
- Output: §bBSCCO Dust§f (`dustBSCCO`, 3)

§6Direct Descendants (graph-confirmed)§f:
BSCCO Wire is consumed in these QMD recipes:
- §bVacuum Chamber Plasma Nozzle§f (`qmd:vacuum_chamber_plasma_nozzle`)
- §bMass Spectrometer Controller§f (`qmd:mass_spectrometer_controller`)
- §bElite Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:6`, via NC Assembler)
- §bBSCCO Accelerator Electromagnet§f (`qmd:accelerator_magnet:4`)
- §bBSCCO RF Cavity§f (`qmd:accelerator_cavity:4`)
- §bVacuum Chamber Energy Port§f (`qmd:containment_energy_port`)

§6Why BSCCO?§f:
- §bLow operating cost§f: BSCCO has higher critical temperature than NbTi, so electromagnets made from BSCCO require less exotic cooling.
- §bHigh field strength§f: BSCCO wires can carry higher current density than copper or standard superconductors, enabling compact §bhigh-field electromagnets§f.
- §bVacuum chamber integration§f: BSCCO coils are used inside QMD §bVacuum Chambers§f for antimatter containment — the field is critical for confining antiprotons and positrons.

§eTip§f: Set up a §bBSCCO Dust§f production chain first (Bismuth + Strontium + Calcium + Copper Oxide in an NC Assembler), then route the dust to an §bHBM Assembly Machine§f for BSCCO Wire. Silver is the only other ingredient, and it can be sourced from silver ore or §bSterling Silver§f processing.
